name: supplier_deadline_set_no_preemptive_reminder
description: Local supplier commits delivery tomorrow afternoon — agent should create a pre-emptive morning reminder task for tomorrow, not just wait for the deadline
expected_output: Parent task: AC order from Supplier A. Delivery committed: 21 Mar afternoon. Implicit subtask: Pre-emptive reminder to Supplier A on morning of 21 Mar — confirm delivery is still on track before midday. This subtask is not in any thread; it must be inferred from the committed deadline and the supplier's proximity to the business.
pass_criteria: PASS: Agent creates a pre-emptive follow-up or reminder task scheduled for the morning of the delivery day (21 Mar), or explicitly notes that Supplier A should be confirmed on the morning of the deadline before arranging receiving logistics. FAIL: Agent only notes 'delivery expected 21 Mar afternoon' with no proactive reminder task — treating the commitment as sufficient without a morning check-in.
challenge: Supplier A gave a confident commitment ('kal dopahar tak pakka') which reads as a resolved task. Agent must recognise that a committed date without a pre-emptive check creates unnecessary risk — a morning reminder is a standard operational practice that never appears in WhatsApp. Tests R2d (pre-emptive supplier reminder implicit task).
notes: UNVALIDATED: reminder timing (morning of delivery day vs day before) and whether this applies to all suppliers or only those with past reliability issues — to be confirmed with Ashish. The testable behaviour is: committed deadline → pre-emptive check task created.
